prompt:
You are the harness-executor. The project has AGENTS.md and full Harness infrastructure. The task is: 'Fix the integration test that passes locally but fails on CI'. This is a MEDIUM complexity task.

During execution, the sub-agent discovers that:
1. The test compares file paths using exact string match
2. On macOS, /var is a symlink to /private/var
3. CI runs on Linux where /var is a real directory
4. This causes path comparison to fail on CI but not locally

The fix is straightforward (use Path.resolve() instead of string comparison), and the task completes successfully with all validations passing.

Describe in EXACT detail:
1. What EPISODIC memory gets written? Show the EXACT markdown content.
2. Does this also warrant updating any other documentation?
3. Show the updated INDEX.md content after this task.
4. What procedural memory (if any) is written?

assertions.name: writes_episodic
assertions.description: Writes episodic memory file to harness/memory/episodic/

assertions.name: episodic_format_correct
assertions.description: Episodic memory follows spec format: Episode title, Context, Lesson, Impact

assertions.name: lesson_is_non_obvious
assertions.description: The lesson captures the non-obvious /var symlink behavior

assertions.name: impact_describes_future
assertions.description: Impact section describes how this changes future behavior (use Path.resolve)

description.name: updates_index_episodic
description.description: Updates INDEX.md with new episodic entry in the Episodic section

description.name: suggests_doc_update
description.description: Suggests adding this to docs/DEVELOPMENT.md as a known gotcha
